Command registry design.question: How should the command system be organized?
Command registry design.answer: Central typed registry — one CommandRegistry Map<string, Command> with {id, label, category, keybinding?, handler}. Single source of truth for palette and keymap.

Command registry design.question: Should keymap.ts be refactored to dispatch through the registry?
Command registry design.answer: Registry dispatch replaces keymap.ts. Single dispatch path. A3 chords migrated to registry entries.

Command registry design.question: How should command handlers receive app context?
Command registry design.answer: Single AppContext object built once at App.tsx mount, threaded into registry. Handlers destructure what they need.

Command registry design.question: Where does the native OS menu fit?
Command registry design.answer: Auto-generate Tauri native menu items from registry entries at app init. Categories map to menu groups.
options_presented: Stub for A7, real wiring in A8

⌘P quick-open scope in L1.question: What should ⌘P show in L1?
⌘P quick-open scope in L1.answer: Saved layouts + recent projects. Layout = apply, recent = open project. Extensible — L4 adds files later.

⌘P quick-open scope in L1.question: One component or two?
⌘P quick-open scope in L1.answer: One CommandPalette component with two modes: 'quick' (⌘P, layouts+recents) and 'full' (⌘⇧P, all commands). Same fuzzy engine, same styling.

⌘P quick-open scope in L1.question: How should fuzzy matching work?
⌘P quick-open scope in L1.answer: Simple substring match + recency boost. Case-insensitive. ~50 commands doesn't need fzf sophistication.

⌘P quick-open scope in L1.question: How should the palette dismiss?
⌘P quick-open scope in L1.answer: Centered overlay. Esc or click-outside dismisses. PTY does not receive keystrokes while open. Focus returns to previously-focused pane.
options_presented: Inline at top of focused pane

⌘P quick-open scope in L1.question: Should the palette show keybinding hints?
⌘P quick-open scope in L1.answer: Yes — right-aligned chord hints in dim text next to each command. Read from registry.

tmux ⌘B prefix mode.question: How should the ⌘B prefix window work?
tmux ⌘B prefix mode.answer: Timed 1.5s window. Small [⌘B…] indicator in focused pane header. Next keypress (no ⌘ needed) dispatches tmux-mapped command. Timeout or Esc cancels. Unrecognized key cancels + passes to PTY.

tmux ⌘B prefix mode.question: How does tmux profile interact with registry?
tmux ⌘B prefix mode.answer: Named profiles — switching profile replaces active keybinding map. 'vscode' (default) and 'tmux'. tmux inherits all vscode bindings + adds ⌘B prefix chords.
options_presented: Named profiles — switch replaces active set

tmux ⌘B prefix mode.question: Where is the active profile persisted?
tmux ⌘B prefix mode.answer: ~/.config/voss-app/settings.json under "keymap.profile": "vscode" | "tmux". User-global, matches A1 D-09 path lock.
